responses.content: Based on the text, why does the Taliban condemn the education of girls and not the education of boys?

My mother used to tell me to hide my face when I spoke to the media because at my age I should be in purdah and she was afraid for my safety. But she never banned me from doing anything. It was a time of horror and fear. People often said the Taliban would kill my father but not me. “Malala is a child,” they would say, “and even the Taliban don’t kill children.”

responses.content: A. “I will get my education if it's at home, school, or somewhere else.”

questions.question: What is most closely a central idea of Vonnegut's letter to the students of Xavier High School?

responses.content: C. Pursuing art for art's sake makes a person's life better.

questions.question: Which sentence from the text most strongly supports the correct answer to the previous question?

responses.content: D. “Practice any art, music, singing, dancing, acting, drawing, painting, sculpting, poetry, fiction, essays, reportage, no matter how well or badly, not to get money and fame, but to experience becoming, to find out what's inside you, to make your soul grow.”

responses.content: This essay will compare and contrast Freedom Walkers and Freedom's Daughters to show that while both authors celebrate everyday heroes of the Civil Rights Movement, they present events differently—Freedman uses the specific timeline of the Montgomery Bus Boycott to show the power of mass protest, while Olson uses personal narratives to emphasize the specific, foundational roles that women played behind the scenes."

In this passage it is going to show how Barbara wanted a change in her school one thing she did is she made assembly and order all the teacher to leave some teachers didn’t leave so she took of her shoe and slammed it on the blaescher and told them to leave they left fast she wanted a change in her school
responses.bucket_rationale: The response shows some understanding of the text and includes a topic sentence and a paraphrase of an event (Barbara Johns making an assembly and telling teachers to leave). However, it only provides one piece of evidence and lacks clear explanations or a second piece of evidence. The details are somewhat accurate but not fully developed or explained, and the structure is incomplete for a full paragraph. Therefore, it fits best in the 'partially-correct-low' category.

questions.question: Review your complete comparative essay one final time. Make any last edits to strengthen your analysis, improve your evidence, or clarify your comparisons before submitting for your final grade. Make these edits in the Final Draft box.

responses.content:
Have you ever wondered how ordinary people helped change history during the Civil Rights Movement?" While comparing and contrasting Freedom Walkers and Freedom's Daughters, we can see that both texts carry the message that grassroots organizing was the backbone of the Civil Rights Movement; however, they develop this differently one through a chronological look at the Montgomery Bus Boycott, and the other by tracing the unrecognized historical The author develops the idea that Claudette's actions In Freedom Walkers, Russell Freedman presents the story of Claudette Colvin to stand up for civil rights And boycott she wanted the bus to be fair in the passage it says bus driver ordered the black teenagers to moveAnother reason is in the passage it says The white boy told Marshall to get up from the seat beside him. Marshall refused. Then the bus driver ordered the black teenagers to move, but they continued to sit where they were they didn’t know the segregation bus laws Another reason is In the passage it says other black riders were arrested and convicted for the same offense-sitting in seats reserved for whites. They paid their fines quietly and continued to ride the public busesthey were upset and wanted a change.  And i te end they did a change to the world Barbara Johns wanted a change and stuck up when teachers wouldnt. In this passage it is going to show how Barbara wanted a change in her school one thing she did is she made assembly and order all the teacher to leave some teachers didn’t leave so she took of her shoe and slammed it on the blaescher and told them to leave they left fast she wanted a change in her school. Another reason is in the book it says 
She say her cool look like a old shack Both Claudette and Barbara stood up against unfair rules—Claudette on the bus and Barbara at her school. These stories matter because they show how young people can make a big difference by standing up for what This is why the to story’s are alike a different and why we should stick up for our rights in both story’s both girls fought for their rights one fought fr bus right while the other one fought for school but either way they are o important either way
responses.bucket_rationale: The paragraph contains several grammar and punctuation mistakes, such as missing punctuation marks, run-on sentences, and incorrect capitalization. These errors go beyond mere style or phrasing suggestions.
